title: One Big Beautiful Bill Act — Senate Final Vote
description: $5T reconciliation bill extending Trump 2017 tax cuts, cutting ~$800B from Medicaid, $350B for border enforcement. Passed 51-50 — VP JD Vance cast tie-breaking vote. Three Republicans (Paul, Tillis, Collins) and all 47 Democrats/Independents voted no.
key_provisions: Permanently extends 2017 TCJA individual and corporate tax rates
Cuts Medicaid $800B+ via work requirements and per-capita caps
$350B for border security and deportation operations
Raises debt ceiling $5T through Jan 2029
Phases out Biden-era clean energy tax credits
Temporary No Tax on Tips and No Tax on Overtime
Rural hospital stabilization fund $50B over 5 years

title: Aerial Firefighting Enhancement Act of 2025
description: Authorizes $2B for new airtankers and helicopters following the record 2024-25 wildfire season. Near-unanimous bipartisan passage.
key_provisions: $2B for new heavy airtankers and helicopters through FY2030
Fast-track procurement bypassing standard Federal Acquisition Regulation
Establishes National Aerial Firefighting Center at Colorado Springs
Minimum standing fleet of 20 large airtankers year-round
Extended pilot duty-hour exemptions during federally declared wildfire emergencies

title: Healthcare Workers Fairness Act
description: Would establish federal minimum nurse-to-patient staffing ratios for hospitals receiving Medicare/Medicaid funding. Modeled on California's 1999 law. No Senate floor vote has occurred.
key_provisions: Min ratios: 1:5 medical-surgical, 1:3 step-down, 1:2 ICU
Applies to all Medicare/Medicaid hospitals
Daily public staffing level posting required in each unit
Prohibits retaliation against reporting nurses
$10,000/day civil penalty after 30-day cure period

title: GENIUS Act — Stablecoin Regulation
description: First federal framework for stablecoins requiring 1:1 dollar-backed reserves and federal licensing. Initial cloture vote failed May 2025 amid concerns about Trump family crypto conflicts-of-interest. No final vote.
key_provisions: 1:1 reserve requirements in cash or short-term US Treasuries
Federal licensing for issuers with $10B+ in circulation
Monthly public reserve disclosures and independent audits
2-year prohibition on new algorithmic stablecoins
AML/KYC requirements for all issuers and custodians

title: Combating Global Corruption Act
description: Would require annual State Department rankings of countries by corruption risk, restrict visas for corrupt foreign officials, and coordinate anti-bribery enforcement with allies. Has bipartisan co-sponsors but no floor vote scheduled.
key_provisions: Annual State Department tiered ranking of countries by corruption risk
Restricts visas for foreign officials credibly accused of corruption or kleptocracy
Coordinates US anti-bribery enforcement with G7 and OECD nations
Treasury tracks foreign officials laundering money through US financial system
Creates Special Envoy for Global Anti-Corruption at Ambassador rank

title: TAKE IT DOWN Act
description: Criminalizes non-consensual intimate deepfakes and requires platforms to remove flagged content within 48 hours. Passed Senate by unanimous consent February 2025. House passed 409-2 April 28. Signed May 19, 2025.
key_provisions: Federal crime to publish non-consensual intimate deepfakes (up to 2 years)
Platforms must remove flagged content within 48 hours of victim notification
Applies to AI-generated and digitally altered imagery
Mandatory restitution for victims from convicted offenders
Civil liability for platforms failing 48-hour removal deadline
donors_for: National Center for Missing & Exploited Children

title: American Data Privacy and Protection Act (ADPPA)
description: Comprehensive national consumer data privacy framework. Would preempt stronger state laws including California CCPA — opposed by 26 state AGs. No floor vote scheduled.
key_provisions: Right to access, correct, delete, and export personal data
Data minimization — collection limited to stated purpose
Targeted ad ban for minors under 17
Preempts most state privacy laws including California CCPA
Private right of action for violations

title: Senate Resolution Condemning DOGE Unauthorized Federal Access
description: Non-binding resolution condemning DOGE's unauthorized access to Treasury and SSA systems. Blocked by Republican majority — never received a floor vote.
key_provisions: Condemns DOGE unauthorized access to Treasury and SSA payment systems
Demands audit of all DOGE system access to federal databases
Calls for DOGE ethics disclosures under OGE rules
Urges DOJ to investigate potential CFAA violations
Declares DOGE activities require congressional authorization

title: Protect Social Security Act
description: Would block DOGE from accessing SSA/Medicare systems without congressional approval and prohibit SSA staffing cuts. Response to DOGE's unauthorized system access. No floor vote.
key_provisions: Prohibits SSA staffing reductions over 5% without congressional authorization
180-day notice before closing any Social Security field office
Bans non-agency personnel from accessing SSA or CMS payment systems
Independent IG audit of any DOGE access to federal benefit databases
Codifies monthly payment schedules as mandatory legal obligations

title: Ending Cartels and Protecting America Act
description: Would designate major drug cartels as Foreign Terrorist Organizations and potentially authorize US military force in Mexico. No floor vote.
key_provisions: Designates Sinaloa Cartel, CJNG, MS-13, Tren de Aragua as FTOs
Enables 18 USC 2339B material support prosecutions
Authorizes US military force against cartels in Mexico
OFAC Treasury sanctions on cartel financial networks
Requires border district US Attorneys to prioritize cartel prosecutions

title: Laken Riley Act
description: Requires mandatory ICE detention for undocumented immigrants charged with theft, burglary, assault on law enforcement, or crimes causing death/bodily injury. Passed 64-35 — 12 Democrats crossed over. First bill signed by President Trump in his second term (Jan 29, 2025).
key_provisions: Mandatory ICE detention for undocumented immigrants charged with theft or burglary
Adds detention for assault on law enforcement (Cornyn Amendment)
Adds detention for crimes causing death or serious bodily injury — Sarah's Law (Ernst Amendment)
Any state AG may sue federal government for non-enforcement
Detention triggered at charge or arrest — no conviction required

title: No Tax on Tips Act
description: Would exempt tip income from federal income tax. Trump 2024 campaign promise estimated to cost $100B+ over 10 years. Folded into One Big Beautiful Bill reconciliation package.
key_provisions: Exempts tip income from federal income tax for service workers
Tips still subject to FICA Social Security and Medicare payroll taxes
5-year sunset provision
Treasury to define eligible occupations by regulation
Does not apply to self-employed contractors
donors_against: Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget

title: SAVE Act — Voter Citizenship Verification
description: Would require documentary proof of US citizenship to register to vote in federal elections. House passed companion version; Senate version stalled below 60-vote filibuster threshold.
key_provisions: Documentary proof of citizenship required for all new federal voter registrations
Accepted documents: US passport, birth certificate, or REAL ID
States must verify existing registrations through SAVE database
Criminal penalties for election officials who fail to enforce
90-day cure period for rejected applicants

title: Kids Online Safety Act (KOSA)
description: Would have required platforms to disable addictive features for minors and mitigate self-harm content. Passed committee 23-1 but never received a 118th Congress floor vote. Not reintroduced in 119th Congress.
key_provisions: Disable infinite scroll, autoplay, push notifications for users under 17 by default
Parental supervision tools for screen time and app access controls
Ban targeted advertising using sensitive data to users under 17
Federal duty of care for platforms regarding harm to minors
FTC enforcement with civil penalties up to $50,000 per violation

title: Inflation Reduction Act
description: $750B reconciliation bill — largest climate investment in US history. Medicare drug price negotiation, ACA subsidy extension, 15% corporate minimum tax. Passed 50-50 with VP Harris tiebreaker. Strict party line — all 50 Republicans voted no. 117th Congress.
key_provisions: $369B for climate and clean energy investments over 10 years
Medicare authorized to negotiate drug prices — 10 drugs beginning 2026
Caps Medicare out-of-pocket drug costs at $2,000/year; insulin at $35/month
15% corporate alternative minimum tax on companies with $1B+ in book profits
3-year extension of ACA marketplace premium subsidies

title: PACT Act — Toxic Exposure for Veterans
description: Largest veterans healthcare expansion in decades — 3.5M veterans exposed to burn pits and Agent Orange. Passed 86-11 after dramatic vote reversal. 117th Congress.
key_provisions: Presumes service connection for 23 burn pit and toxic exposure conditions including 9 cancers
Extends VA healthcare eligibility to all post-9/11 combat veterans for 10 years
Adds hypertension and monoclonal gammopathy to Agent Orange presumptive conditions
$282B mandatory VA spending over 10 years for toxic-exposed veterans
Toxic exposure screening for all new VA patients
donors_for: Iraq and Afghanistan Veterans of America

title: Bipartisan Safer Communities Act
description: First major federal gun safety legislation in nearly 30 years. Passed 65-33 with 15 Republican votes after the Uvalde school shooting. Expanded background checks, funded red-flag laws, closed boyfriend loophole. 117th Congress.
key_provisions: Enhanced background checks for gun buyers under age 21 — includes juvenile mental health records
Closes 'boyfriend loophole' — domestic violence prohibition extended to dating partners
$750M grants for state red-flag (crisis intervention) law programs
Clarifies definition of federally licensed gun dealer to reduce unlicensed sales
Funds school mental health services and community violence intervention programs

title: Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (IIJA)
description: $1.2T bipartisan infrastructure law. Passed 69-30 with 19 Republicans crossing over. 117th Congress.
key_provisions: $110B for roads, bridges, and major infrastructure repair
$66B for passenger and freight rail including Amtrak expansion
$65B to expand broadband access to unserved communities
$55B for water infrastructure and lead pipe replacement
$73B for power grid upgrades and clean energy transmission

title: American Rescue Plan Act
description: $1.9T COVID relief — $1,400 stimulus checks, expanded Child Tax Credit, $350B for states and localities. Passed 50-49 strictly party-line — zero Republican votes. 117th Congress.
key_provisions: $1,400 direct stimulus payments to most individuals
Expanded Child Tax Credit to $3,000–$3,600 per child, fully refundable for 1 year
$350B for state, local, territorial, and tribal government fiscal relief
$160B for vaccine distribution, testing, and contact tracing
$300/week federal unemployment supplement through September 2021

title: CARES Act — COVID-19 Economic Relief
description: $2.2T emergency COVID-19 relief — largest economic stimulus in US history at the time. Passed 96-0. Only Rand Paul voted no. Several members missed the vote. 116th Congress.
key_provisions: $1,200 direct payments to adults ($500 per qualifying child)
$500B corporate stabilization fund including $25B for airlines
$349B Paycheck Protection Program — forgivable SBA loans for small businesses
$600/week federal unemployment supplement through July 2020
$100B for hospitals, healthcare providers, and emergency medical supply stockpiling

title: First Step Act
description: Bipartisan criminal justice reform — reduced mandatory minimums, expanded early release, reformed federal prisons. Passed 87-12. Rare coalition of Koch Industries and ACLU. 115th Congress.
key_provisions: Reduces mandatory minimums for nonviolent drug offenders with prior convictions
Expands good-time credit from 47 to 54 days per year for federal prisoners
Requires inmates be housed within 500 miles of primary residence when space permits
Bans shackling of pregnant federal inmates during labor and delivery
Creates risk-and-needs assessment system for prisoner rehabilitation programming
donors_against: National Association of Assistant US Attorneys

title: Border Security, Economic Opportunity, and Immigration Modernization Act
description: Comprehensive immigration reform passed Senate 68-32 with 14 Republican votes. 13-year path to citizenship for ~11M undocumented immigrants plus border security surge. Died in House — Speaker Boehner never brought it to a floor vote. 113th Congress.
key_provisions: 13-year path to citizenship for ~11 million undocumented immigrants
$46B border security surge — 700-mile fence completion, 20,000 new Border Patrol agents
New W-visa program for agricultural and low-skilled workers
H-1B visa cap doubled from 65,000 to 130,000
Mandatory E-Verify for all employers within 5 years

title: One Big Beautiful Bill Act — House Final Vote
description: House concurred in Senate amendments 218-214. Two Republicans (Thomas Massie, Brian Fitzpatrick) joined all 212 Democrats in opposition. Signed July 4, 2025.
key_provisions: Permanently extends 2017 TCJA tax cuts — estimated $4.5T cost over 10 years (CBO)
Cuts Medicaid ~$800B via work requirements, per-capita caps, and provider tax limits
Eliminates SNAP for ~8 million households via tighter work requirements
$350B surge for border security, ICE, and deportation operations
Raises debt ceiling $5 trillion through January 2029

title: TAKE IT DOWN Act — House Vote
description: House passed companion to Senate's S.146. Vote was 409-2 with 22 not voting. Only Thomas Massie (R-KY) and Eric Burlison (R-MO) voted no.
key_provisions: Federal crime to publish non-consensual intimate deepfakes
Platforms must remove flagged content within 48 hours
Applies to all user-generated content platforms
Criminal penalties up to 2 years imprisonment plus restitution
Federal civil cause of action for victims against non-compliant platforms
donors_for: National Center for Missing & Exploited Children

title: Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act 2025
description: CR funding the federal government through September 2025. Passed 217-213 mostly along party lines. Freedom Caucus hardliners opposed as insufficiently cutting; small number of Democrats supported.
key_provisions: Funds government through September 30, 2025 at FY2024 levels
~1% across-the-board cut to non-defense discretionary spending
Reauthorizes FISA Section 702 surveillance for one additional year
$6B emergency supplemental for disaster relief
No new policy riders on DEI, abortion, or immigration

title: No Rogue Rulings Act
description: Passed House along party lines. Would prohibit single federal district judges from issuing nationwide injunctions. Response to judges blocking Trump executive orders.
key_provisions: Prohibits US district courts from issuing nationwide injunctions
Limits injunctive relief to named plaintiffs before that specific court
Requires en banc appellate review before any nationwide stay of executive action
30-day expedited timeline for appellate review of government actions
Applies retroactively to pending injunctions against executive branch actions

title: EACH Act — Equal Access to Abortion Coverage in Health Insurance
description: Would permanently repeal the Hyde Amendment, requiring Medicaid and all federal programs to cover abortion. No House floor vote has been scheduled.
key_provisions: Permanently repeals the Hyde Amendment and all similar appropriations restrictions
Requires Medicaid to cover abortion with full federal-state matching funds
Mandates ACA tax-credit plans cover abortion services
Covers all federal employee health benefit plans including TRICARE
Prohibits states from restricting abortion coverage in federally-funded programs

title: No Taxpayer Funding for Abortion Act
description: Permanently codifies the Hyde Amendment in statute — banning federal abortion funding across all programs. Passes House in early weeks of each Republican-led Congress; typically dies in Senate.
key_provisions: Permanently codifies Hyde Amendment ban as permanent statute (not annual appropriations rider)
Applies to Medicaid, CHIP, Medicare, ACA marketplace plans, and federal employee benefits
Allows exceptions only for rape, incest, or life of the mother
Prohibits ACA premium tax credits for insurance plans covering elective abortion
Codifies Helms Amendment restricting foreign assistance for abortion internationally

title: REINS Act
description: Would require Congress to affirmatively approve any 'major rule' ($100M+ economic impact). Has passed House every Congress since 2011; never cleared the Senate.
key_provisions: Major rules ($100M+ economic impact) require congressional approval within 70 session days
Rule automatically expires without affirmative joint resolution
Applies to EPA, CFPB, OSHA, FDA, FCC, and all executive agencies
Creates privileged floor procedure for expedited review in both chambers
Exempts emergency rules and rules implementing court orders

title: Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act
description: Requires healthcare providers to give the same standard of care to infants born alive after attempted abortion as to any other newborn. Passed party-line — Democrats argue the 2002 Born-Alive Infants Protection Act already covers this.
key_provisions: Requires immediate medical care for any infant born alive after attempted abortion
Applies regardless of whether birth occurred during or after an abortion procedure
Mandates immediate hospital transfer for born-alive infants
Up to 5 years criminal penalty for non-compliant practitioners
Private right of action for women and infants against non-compliant providers

title: Abolish the Department of Education Act
description: One-sentence bill to abolish the US Department of Education by December 31, 2026. Trump endorsed closing DOE during 2024 campaign. No House floor vote — leadership prefers executive action.
key_provisions: Abolishes the US Department of Education effective December 31, 2026
No transition provisions, replacement mechanisms, or funding alternatives provided
Affects $80B+ annual education budget distribution
Student loan servicing (FSA/FAFSA) unresolved
Special education IDEA funding enforcement unresolved

title: Censure of President Biden — Classified Documents
description: Non-binding resolution censuring Biden for mishandling classified documents per the Hur report. Failed on party-line vote. Several moderate Republicans also voted no.
key_provisions: Formally censures President Biden for mishandling classified documents
Cites Hur report finding Biden 'willfully retained' classified materials
Expresses sense of House that Biden should have been charged
Calls on National Archives to audit Biden-era document irregularities
Non-binding — no legal effect on Biden's pension or retirement benefits

title: Protecting American Energy Production Act
description: Prohibits the President from declaring a climate emergency without congressional approval and restricts executive limitations on oil and gas production. Passed House party-line.
key_provisions: Prohibits restricting oil/gas production via emergency declaration
Requires congressional authorization for presidential climate emergency declarations
Codifies elimination of Biden-era LNG export pause
Rolls back NEPA environmental review requirements for energy permits
Directs BLM to process pending oil and gas permits within 30 days

title: Antisemitism Awareness Act
description: Requires DOE to use the IHRA definition of antisemitism when enforcing Title VI on campuses. Passed 320-91 with strong bipartisan support amid campus protests. 118th Congress.
key_provisions: Adopts IHRA definition of antisemitism for DOE Title VI enforcement
IHRA definition includes certain anti-Israel criticism as potentially antisemitic
Directs DOE to investigate campus complaints using broader IHRA definition
No new private right of action for individuals
Applies to all institutions receiving federal education funding

title: FAA Reauthorization Act of 2024
description: 5-year FAA reauthorization passed 387-26 with overwhelming bipartisan support. Addresses Boeing safety crisis, air traffic controller shortage, and passenger rights. 118th Congress.
key_provisions: 5-year FAA reauthorization through September 2028
New Boeing manufacturing oversight office with direct FAA inspector presence
Addresses air traffic controller shortage — 3,000 positions below staffing target
Mandatory automatic cash refunds for flight cancellations
Increased FAA safety inspector workforce with dedicated Boeing audit team

title: National Security Supplemental — Ukraine/Israel/Taiwan Aid
description: $95B foreign aid package. Ukraine $61B, Israel $26B, Taiwan $8B. Passed 311-112 with mostly Democratic and some Republican support. Freedom Caucus almost entirely opposed. 118th Congress.
key_provisions: $61B military and economic aid for Ukraine — HIMARS, artillery, Javelin missiles
$26B for Israel including Iron Dome and David's Sling replenishment
$8B for Indo-Pacific security including Taiwan arms sales acceleration
Authorizes seizure and transfer of $300B in frozen Russian sovereign assets
Includes TikTok divestiture provision

title: TikTok Divestiture Act (PAFACA)
description: Forces ByteDance to divest TikTok within 270 days or face US app store and web hosting ban. Passed 352-65. Signed into law. TikTok went dark briefly Jan 19, 2025. 118th Congress.
key_provisions: ByteDance must divest TikTok US operations within 270 days
President can extend deadline once by 90 days if divestiture is in good-faith progress
App stores and web hosts must remove non-compliant apps or face civil penalties
Applies to apps with 1M+ US monthly users controlled by foreign adversary nations
Civil penalties up to $5,000 per US user per day for violations

title: Secure the Border Act
description: Sweeping House border bill passing 219-213 party-line. Trump pressured Senate Republicans to reject the bipartisan Lankford-Murphy compromise that included many of these provisions. Never received a Senate floor vote. 118th Congress.
key_provisions: Resumes and accelerates southern border wall construction
Ends catch-and-release — mandatory detention of all border crossers
Restricts asylum eligibility exclusively to those arriving at ports of entry
Adds 100 new immigration court judges and support staff
Expands expedited removal authority across entire US

title: Fiscal Responsibility Act — Debt Ceiling Deal
description: Suspended debt ceiling through Jan 2025 — averted first-ever US default. Passed 314-117 with bipartisan support; Freedom Caucus and progressive Squad both largely opposed. 118th Congress.
key_provisions: Suspends debt limit through January 1, 2025 — averts US sovereign default
Caps non-defense discretionary spending growth at 1%/year through FY2025
Claws back $28B in unspent COVID-19 relief appropriations
Rescinds $1.4B in IRS enforcement funding from IRA
Adds SNAP work requirements for recipients ages 50-54

title: Parents Bill of Rights Act
description: Passed 213-208 almost entirely along party lines. Requires schools to publish curricula and library books; mandates parental notification before changing student gender at school. Died in Democratic Senate. 118th Congress.
key_provisions: Requires schools to publish complete curricula and materials lists accessible to all parents
Mandates parental notification before changing a student's name, pronouns, or gender at school
Requires school libraries to disclose all titles and allow parental opt-out
Gives parents right to attend school board meetings and review annual budgets
Prohibits graphic sexual content in school instructional materials under Title I

title: Inflation Reduction Act — House Final Passage
description: House final passage 220-207 strict party-line. Largest climate investment in US history, Medicare drug pricing reform, 15% corporate minimum tax. 117th Congress.
key_provisions: $369B for climate and clean energy over 10 years
Medicare authorized to negotiate drug prices beginning 2026
EV tax credits up to $7,500 for qualifying vehicles
15% corporate alternative minimum tax on companies with $1B+ in book income
$80B additional IRS enforcement funding (partially rescinded in 2023)

title: Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (TCJA)
description: $1.5T tax overhaul — largest since 1986. Permanent 21% corporate rate, temporary individual cuts through 2025, $10K SALT cap, 20% pass-through deduction. Passed 224-201 party-line. 115th Congress.
key_provisions: Permanently cuts corporate tax rate from 35% to 21%
Temporarily reduces individual income tax rates across all brackets through 2025
Nearly doubles standard deduction ($12,000 single / $24,000 MFJ)
Caps SALT deduction at $10,000 — impacts high-tax state residents significantly
Creates 20% deduction for qualified pass-through business income (Section 199A)

title: 21st Century Cures Act
description: Bipartisan legislation accelerating FDA drug and device approvals, funding Biden's cancer moonshot and BRAIN initiative NIH research, mental health reform, and opioid treatment programs. Passed 392-26 overwhelmingly. 114th Congress.
key_provisions: $4.8B over 10 years for NIH research including cancer moonshot and BRAIN Initiative
Streamlines FDA drug approval using real-world evidence alongside clinical trial data
$1B in grants for opioid addiction prevention, treatment, and recovery support
Mental health parity enforcement and expansion of inpatient psychiatric capacity
Authorizes FDA to consider patient experience data in drug approval decisions

title: Affordable Care Act (ACA / Obamacare)
description: Landmark healthcare reform — insurance exchanges, Medicaid expansion, pre-existing condition protections, individual mandate. Passed 219-212 with zero Republican votes. 111th Congress.
key_provisions: Created health insurance exchanges in all 50 states with income-based subsidies
Expanded Medicaid eligibility to 138% of the federal poverty level (optional for states)
Banned denial of coverage for pre-existing conditions and ended lifetime limits
Required insurers to cover dependents until age 26
Individual mandate (repealed by TCJA 2017)

title: FY2027 Reconciliation Act — Senate Passage
description: Senate passed S.2, the FY2027 budget reconciliation bill, 52–47 — the second major Republican reconciliation package of the 119th Congress following the One Big Beautiful Bill (H.R.1, Jul 2025). Lisa Murkowski (R-AK) was the only Republican to vote no. All 45 Senate Democrats and both Independents (King, Sanders) voted against.
key_provisions: Extends and expands 2017 TCJA tax cuts beyond H.R.1 provisions
Additional Medicaid spending caps and work requirement expansions
Further rollback of IRA clean energy tax credits
FY2027 discretionary spending reductions across domestic programs
Continued border and immigration enforcement funding
SNAP/food stamp eligibility tightening
Debt ceiling adjustment for FY2027 fiscal year
